classification.maturity: validated via hardware-in-the-loop with OPAL-RT simulator
classification.domain: power system protection instrumentation, cyber-physical security

topic.what: AI-based measurement integrity validation scheme (MIVS) using RNNs to detect false-data injection attacks (FDIAs) targeting AC and DC line current differential relays (LCDRs) in islanded inverter-based microgrids
topic.how: Interprets short windows of synchronized instantaneous current measurements exploiting temporal structure to assess physical consistency and distinguish genuine fault-induced trajectories from cyber-manipulated measurement streams without requiring additional hardware
topic.why_matters: In inverter-based microgrids fault currents are actively limited by converter controls making magnitude-based and phasor-based detection ineffective; FDIAs on LCDRs can cause false trips leading to microgrid instability and service disruption

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of correctly classified FDIA and fault cases for AC LCDR
characteristics.context: Tested on 20% holdout dataset with 1000 fault and 1000 FDIA scenarios

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of correctly classified FDIA and fault cases for DC LCDR
characteristics.context: Tested on 20% holdout dataset with 600 fault and 600 FDIA scenarios

characteristics.what_it_measures: End-to-end latency from LCDR pickup to MIVS decision output
characteristics.evidence: Fig. 13 and Section VI-A HIL validation
characteristics.context: Real-time HIL validation using OPAL-RT OP5700 simulator at 1 kHz sampling
